title: Renting to Low Income Tenants
content:
# Renting to Low Income Tenants

Find information about renting your property to low income tenants and the benefits you can receive.

Toggle

Page Sections

## Income Restricted Rentals: Section 8

Section 8 housing is restricted to households earning a certain amount of money.

Renting to a family through the Section 8 Housing Voucher Program offers many benefits to landlords. The program is federally funded and administered by the Boston Housing Authority (BHA). Once approved, the BHA will help you screen potential tenants and advertise your unit. Vouchers are delivered straight to you (the landlord) rather than through the tenant. Visit the BHA website for complete information.

Section 8 information

## Renting to the Previously Homeless

The Landlord Incentive Program provides benefits to landlords who rent to Boston households who are transitioning out of homelessness.

Receive ongoing benefits and support as a landlord renting through the Landlord Incentive Program.

**BENEFITS TO LANDLORDS**

* Signing bonuses
* Payment for holding units during screening
* Cleaning fees
* Ongoing dedicated services for tenant
* One number to call for referrals and during unit occupancy

Landlord Incentive Program
